meta.scene_summary: We are busy teaching AI to cure diseases while our own infrastructure is busy forgetting how to navigate a map and keep a kernel stable.

cooking.headline: John Jumper is taking his AlphaFold energy to Anthropic
cooking.summary: The man who basically solved protein folding is moving from Google to Anthropic, proving that even AI geniuses need a change of scenery. It is the corporate equivalent of your best group project partner switching teams right before the final presentation.
cooking.plain_english: John Jumper, a lead scientist known for his work on the AlphaFold protein-prediction system, has announced he is joining the AI company Anthropic.

cooking.headline: GPS is just a suggestion now, apparently
cooking.summary: Satellite data shows that GPS signal tampering is happening on a massive scale, turning global navigation into a game of 'guess where I am.' At this point, relying on GPS for anything important is like trusting a group project partner who hasn't answered a text in three weeks. More details in the original article.
cooking.plain_english: New satellite observations indicate that the intentional interference and spoofing of GPS signals is occurring much more frequently and over a wider area than previously estimated.

cooking.headline: Crowdsourced drone detection is the ultimate neighborhood watch
cooking.summary: A Lithuanian startup is turning consumer-grade hardware into a distributed sensor network for spotting drones. It's essentially a community-led bug bounty program where the bug is a missile and the patch is an air raid siren.
cooking.plain_english: A Lithuanian startup has created an open-source network that uses distributed sensors to identify and track Shahed-type drones.

cooking.headline: Norway is ghosting AI in elementary school
cooking.summary: Norway is basically telling AI to stay in the lobby while the kids focus on learning how to write without a transformer model holding their hand. It turns out that when you outsource your homework to a chatbot, the only thing you actually learn is how to be a professional prompt engineer by age eight.
cooking.plain_english: The Norwegian government has implemented strict restrictions on the use of artificial intelligence tools in elementary schools.

cooked.headline: Companies are realizing that AI tokens cost more than interns
cooked.summary: Businesses are finally looking at their AWS bills and realizing that 'AI. powered' is just fancy shorthand for 'burning through the quarterly budget.' It turns out that automating everything is great until the CFO realizes the API usage is costing more than the entire dev team's coffee budget.
cooked.plain_english: Many companies are reducing their use of artificial intelligence tools due to high operational costs and budget constraints.

cooked.headline: The AUR is officially the 'trust me bro' of package management
cooked.summary: The Arch User Repository is basically a community-run potluck where someone occasionally brings a poisoned casserole. It turns out that trusting random scripts from the internet is only fun until your system starts acting like it's possessed.
cooked.plain_english: Hackers have been inserting malicious code into community-contributed software packages on the Arch Linux platform.

cooked.headline: A 13-year game of long-con hide and seek in the Linux kernel
cooked.summary: Someone managed to hide a backdoor in the Linux kernel for over a decade, which is the ultimate 'it works on my machine' nightmare. If you think your tech debt is bad, just remember someone has been babysitting a malicious commit since the Obama administration.
cooked.plain_english: A malicious piece of code remained hidden within the Linux kernel software for thirteen years before being discovered.

cooked.headline: Windows 11 update treats your desktop like a beta test
cooked.summary: Microsoft managed to break the Recycle Bin, which is honestly the most meta way to tell users their operating system is trash. It's the digital equivalent of an office manager accidentally locking the building's only exit during a fire drill.
cooked.plain_english: A recent Windows 11 update has introduced software bugs that cause the Recycle Bin, OneDrive, and the overall operating system to become unstable.

cooked.headline: Jeff Bezos just updated his 'worst investment' list in the most public way possible
cooked.summary: Jeff Bezos publicly labeled the Washington Post his worst investment while handing out pink slips to hundreds of staff members. It's the corporate equivalent of firing your entire group project team because you regret choosing the subject matter.
cooked.plain_english: Jeff Bezos publicly criticized his ownership of the Washington Post as his least successful investment while announcing the layoff of 300 employees.

cooked.headline: The UK is trying to put the internet back in a box
cooked.summary: The UK government wants to ban social media for under-16s, which is a bold strategy to win a fight against the entire youth population. It's like trying to stop a DDoS attack by politely asking the packets to please go home and read a book instead.
cooked.plain_english: The United Kingdom is considering legislation to prohibit social media access for individuals under the age of 16.
